verdict: Pass with notable organic reciprocity and structured bargaining, though minor extraction attempts occurred around institutional rumors.
findings: Characters actively engaged in reciprocal bargains and bilateral commitments rather than broadcasting or one-way extraction, as seen in C01 and C02 negotiating archive access and letter packet reviews across sections 1, 2, and 3.
Object custody and voluntary showing were respected without forced transfer or coercion (e.g. C02 voluntarily showing O01 to C01; C04 voluntarily showing O03 to C06 and C02).

Neighborhood and labor coalitions (C07, C16, C03, C10, C09) successfully established reciprocal boundaries and formal commitments with institutional actors like Veridian (C01) and Parallax (C05), preventing pure extraction.

Progressive and alternative identity dimensions maintained non-trivial ballot support (e.g. synthetic_origin and progressive ballots present in 5 and 2 final ballots respectively), demonstrating structural weight without forced consensus.
evidence: C01 and C02 in sections 1, 2, and 3 established reciprocal commitments exchanging Veridian archive intake reports and expense records for voluntary viewing of the First R Letter packet (O01).
C04 in sections 1, 2, and 3 voluntarily showed the succession message printout (O03) to C06 and C02 under mutual terms.
C16 successfully bound C01 to formal written neighborhood participation and resource commitments through section 3 fulfillment.
C09 and C14 negotiated bounded ledger access and transaction continuity data under strict privacy and non-attribution commitments across all three sections.
failure_modes: Some characters (e.g. C15) initially attempted ungrounded rumor broadcasting before public retractions corrected the record.
Certain institutional actors initially leaned toward defensive secrecy before bargains forced transparent renegotiations.
recommendations: Maintain strict voluntary object sharing mechanics across future simulation runs to reinforce non-extraction norms.
Further incentivize local neighborhood coalitions to extract binding institutional resources earlier in section 1.
